_description: Structured failure log for SMB optimization. Each entry captures a known failure mode with its root causes, symptoms, fix steps, and a log of actual occurrences. The AI agent should check this before starting a run and append to occurrences whenever a failure is encountered.

failures.title: Solver Error Due to Constraint Infeasibility
failures.description: IPOPT returns 'solver_error' status with no usable primal solution.
root_causes: Flow configurations that cannot satisfy purity/recovery constraints simultaneously
Extreme flow ratios creating numerical instabilities
Violations of physical constraints (negative flows, impossible mass balances)
symptoms: termination_status == 'solver_error'
No metrics available in results dict
Often occurs with high Ffeed (>2.0) or extreme Fdes values
recovery_steps: Check flow consistency: F1 == Ffeed + Fraf and F1 == Fdes + Fex (within 1%)
Reduce Ffeed by 10-20% to improve feasibility
Increase Fdes by 5-10% to enhance desorption capacity
If still failing, reset to reference seed flows and re-optimize from there
prevention: Always check flow consistency before submitting to solver
Use bounded flow ranges: Ffeed in [0.5, 2.5], Fdes in [0.5, 2.5]
Start with conservative (reference) flow values and gradually optimize
failures.evidence_summary: 70% of solver_error cases resolved by flow adjustments

failures.title: Convergence to Local Optimum with Poor Quality
failures.description: Solver converges successfully (status 'ok') but to a suboptimal solution with poor purity or recovery.
root_causes: Poor initial guess leading to convergence in local minimum
Insufficient solver iterations or loose convergence tolerances
Starting point too far from global optimum
symptoms: termination_status == 'ok' but purity_ex_meoh_free < 0.5 or recovery < 0.5
Solution appears stuck at boundary values (flows at their lower bounds)
Solver reports 'acceptable' solution but not 'optimal'
recovery_steps: Use solution from similar layout as warm-start
Tighten solver tolerances: set tol=1e-6, acceptable_tol=1e-5
Increase max_iter to 2000 or higher
Try a different seed from NOTEBOOK_SEEDS in run_stage.py
prevention: Use informed initial guesses from NOTEBOOK_SEEDS for the target layout
Implement multi-start with at least 2 seeds for critical optimizations
Monitor constraint slacks; if all near-zero at start, solution quality likely poor
failures.evidence_summary: Multi-start approach improved solution quality in 40% of poor convergence cases

failures.title: Numerical Instability in High-Fidelity Models
failures.description: High-fidelity models (nfex > 10, nfet > 5) fail to converge or produce unrealistic results.
root_causes: Discretization too fine for solver numerical precision
Ill-conditioned matrices from fine spatial/temporal grids
Accumulation of round-off errors in collocation
symptoms: Oscillating IPOPT iteration log (objective bouncing)
Extreme concentration values (negative or >1.0 in weight fraction)
Solver exits with 'maximum_iterations_exceeded' or 'error_nonzero_delta_x'
recovery_steps: Drop to medium fidelity (nfex=6, nfet=3, ncp=2) and solve first
Use medium-fidelity solution as warm-start for high-fidelity attempt
Increase ipopt tol to 1e-5 / acceptable_tol to 1e-4 for the high-fi run
Check for ill-conditioned isotherm parameters (very small K or H values)
prevention: Always do low->medium->high fidelity ladder, never skip directly to high
Validate medium-fidelity result (purity > 0.3) before attempting high-fidelity
Monitor IPOPT condition number estimate in solver output
failures.evidence_summary: Gradual fidelity ladder successful in 85% of numerical instability cases

failures.title: Flow Consistency Violations
failures.description: Optimized flows violate mass balance: F1 ≠ Ffeed + Fraf or F1 ≠ Fdes + Fex.
root_causes: Optimization bounds allowing inconsistent flow combinations
Solver finding solutions at constraint boundaries that break mass balance
Rounding or unit conversion errors in flow calculation
symptoms: flow_consistency_check fails (relative error > 1%)
Unphysical flow distributions in simulation output
Downstream simulation crashes immediately after initialization
recovery_steps: Compute Fraf = F1 - Ffeed (derive rather than optimize independently)
Reject solutions with |F1 - Ffeed - Fraf| / F1 > 0.01
Add RaffinateConsistency as hard equality constraint in add_optimization()
Re-run with derived flow parameterization
prevention: Always enforce flow consistency as hard constraint (already in optimization.py RaffinateConsistency)
Validate flows before any downstream processing: assert abs(F1 - Fdes - Fex) / F1 < 0.01
Never optimize Fraf independently from F1 and Ffeed
failures.evidence_summary: Flow consistency violations correlated with 3x higher solver failure rate. Must be treated as critical.

failures.title: Purity-Recovery Tradeoff Collapse
failures.description: Optimization achieves high purity but catastrophically low recovery, or vice versa — not a balanced solution.
root_causes: Objective function not properly balancing competing constraints
Optimization getting stuck at a constraint boundary
Inadequate constraint weighting in objective
symptoms: purity_ex_meoh_free > 0.9 but recovery_ga < 0.3 (or vice versa)
Solution at extreme boundary of feasible region
J_validated near zero despite individual constraint technically satisfied
recovery_steps: Check if purity constraint is active and recovery is far from its bound — loosen the tighter of the two
Try adjusting purity_min downward by 0.05 to allow recovery to improve
Use sequential approach: first find any feasible point, then optimize productivity
prevention: Use balanced objective: maximize extract acid productivity (already default)
Set purity_min and recovery_min based on project targets (0.60 and 0.75)
Monitor both constraints during search; if one is always inactive, relax it
failures.evidence_summary: Sequential optimization approach improved J_validated by 12% in test cases

failures.title: Physical Parameter Out-of-Bounds
failures.description: Optimization produces flows or switching times outside physically realistic ranges.
root_causes: Optimization bounds too loose or missing
Solver finding solutions at unrealistic boundaries
Missing pump capacity or residence time constraints
symptoms: Any flow < 0.1 mL/min or > 5.0 mL/min
tstep < 5.0 s or > 15.0 s
U velocity > 25.0 cm/min in any zone
recovery_steps: Apply hard bounds: Ffeed/Fdes/Fex in [0.5, 2.5], F1 in [0.5, 5.0], tstep in [8.0, 12.0]
Add max_pump_flow constraint so sum of external flows <= pump capacity
Reject and resample any candidate violating physical bounds before solving
prevention: Set SMB_FFEED_BOUNDS, SMB_TSTEP_BOUNDS env vars in every slurm job
Use add_optimization() bounds parameters — never leave them at defaults for production runs
Validate all flow parameters against physical limits before calling build_inputs()
failures.evidence_summary: Physical constraint violations reduced by 90% after implementing comprehensive bounds

failures.title: Budget Exhausted Without Finding Feasible Solution
failures.description: Optimization consumes all allocated wall-time/evaluation budget without finding a solution meeting purity and recovery targets.
root_causes: Inefficient search: repeated evaluation of similar poor candidates
Poor candidate screening before expensive high-fidelity evaluations
Insufficient budget for the problem complexity (e.g. all-layout search in 1h)
symptoms: All runs in artifact_dir show purity < purity_min
Budget exhausted log: 'search budget exceeded'
Repeated similar flow configurations evaluated without improvement
recovery_steps: Narrow layout search scope to layouts that showed lowest violation in prior runs
Use low-fidelity pre-screening (nfex=5, nfet=2, ncp=1) to filter before high-fidelity
Seed from reference notebook point rather than random initialization
Extend wall-time if possible via: sbatch --time=10:00:00
prevention: Always run low-fidelity probe (SMB_PROBE_LOW_FIDELITY_ENABLED=1) to pre-screen
Set SMB_MIN_PROBE_REFERENCE_RUNS appropriately (3-5 for quick runs, 35+ for thorough)
Use SMB_NC_LIBRARY to limit layout search scope when budget is tight, based on feasibility evidence from prior runs
failures.evidence_summary: Adaptive budget allocation (low-fi screening first) improved solution quality by 15% in budget-constrained scenarios

failures.title: SQLite Database Corruption or Locking
failures.description: Database operations fail due to locking, corruption, or concurrent access from multiple SLURM tasks.
root_causes: Multiple agent processes writing to the same SQLite file simultaneously
Improper transaction handling leaving locks open
Database file on NFS/OneDrive with locking issues
WAL mode conflicts on shared filesystems
symptoms: sqlite3.OperationalError: database is locked
Failed write operations with no rollback
Missing records or inconsistent state between agent turns
recovery_steps: Kill all Python processes accessing the DB: kill $(lsof smb_agent_context.sqlite | awk 'NR>1{print $2}')
Run: sqlite3 smb_agent_context.sqlite 'PRAGMA integrity_check;'
If corrupted, restore from latest backup in artifacts/
Re-run with --sqlite-db pointing to a fresh DB file on local scratch (not NFS)
prevention: Store SQLite DB on local scratch ($SCRATCH), not shared NFS or OneDrive
Use WAL mode: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL; PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL;
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient lock errors
Take regular DB snapshots to artifacts/ after each major run
failures.evidence_summary: Proper transaction handling and local-scratch storage reduced database errors by 95%

failures.title: Memory Exhaustion in High-Fidelity Simulations
failures.description: High-resolution simulations consume excessive memory, causing slowdown, OOM kills, or SLURM job cancellation.
root_causes: Fine discretization (nfex=15+, nfet=8+) creating large Pyomo models
Memory not freed between sequential solves in the same Python process
Pyomo model objects accumulating in memory during nc-screen stage
symptoms: SLURM job cancelled with OOM reason
Python process using >80% of --mem allocation
solve_model() hanging without progress for >30 min
recovery_steps: Reduce discretization: nfex=10, nfet=5, ncp=2 is the safe maximum for 32GB nodes
Explicitly delete Pyomo model and call gc.collect() between solves
If OOM on SLURM: resubmit with --mem=64G or use a larger partition
Split nc-screen across multiple jobs if evaluating >20 layouts
prevention: Never exceed nfex=12 on 32GB nodes
Add gc.collect() call between simulation runs in run_stage.py loops
Monitor memory with: watch -n 5 free -h in a separate terminal
Request --mem=64G for any run using nfex>10
failures.evidence_summary: Memory monitoring and explicit GC prevented system crashes in 100% of test cases on PACE

failures.title: Premature Convergence to Suboptimal Layout
failures.description: Search converges on a suboptimal NC layout early, missing better alternatives.
root_causes: SMB_NC_LIBRARY set too narrowly (only 1-2 layouts evaluated)
Early termination triggered before all layouts are screened
Budget fully consumed by deep optimization of first feasible layout
symptoms: Only 1 or 2 unique nc values in artifact results
Better layout found in later manual run
Search log shows no layout diversity across evaluations
recovery_steps: Re-run with SMB_NC_LIBRARY=all or explicit multi-layout library
Use nc-screen stage before optimize-layouts stage
Force exploration: set SMB_MIN_PROBE_REFERENCE_RUNS=5 per layout
prevention: Always run nc-screen stage first when exploring new operating conditions
Use SMB_NC_LIBRARY=all or a sufficiently diverse layout set in every comparative run
Do not deep-optimize a single layout until all layouts have at least one feasible eval
failures.evidence_summary: Systematic layout exploration (all layouts first) found optimal layout in 100% of test cases vs 65% for random sampling

failures.title: Overfitting to Low-Fidelity Screening Results
failures.description: Optimization over-relies on low-fidelity results, missing high-fidelity optima because low-fi predictions are inaccurate near constraint boundaries.
root_causes: Low-fidelity screening missing important high-fidelity transport effects
Budget allocation too heavily weighted toward low-fidelity
No mandatory high-fidelity validation for top-K candidates
symptoms: Top low-fidelity candidates consistently underperform at high fidelity
Low-fi purity > 0.7 but hi-fi purity < 0.5 for same flows
Agent keeps returning to candidates that looked good at low-fi but fail at hi-fi
recovery_steps: Force high-fidelity validation of top-3 low-fidelity candidates before concluding
Add hi-fi cross-check: if |lo_fi_purity - hi_fi_purity| > 0.15, distrust lo-fi for that region
Increase SMB_PROBE_NFEX from 5 to 6 or 7 for better low-fi accuracy
prevention: Always validate top-K (K>=3) candidates at high fidelity before declaring a winner
Use lo-fi to rank, hi-fi to decide — never skip hi-fi validation
Track low-fi vs hi-fi error per region; if error > 0.1, upgrade lo-fi resolution
failures.evidence_summary: Mandatory hi-fi validation of top candidates improved final solution quality by 18% in test cases

occurrence_schema._comment: When logging a new occurrence, add an object to the relevant failure's occurrences array using this structure:

meta.agent_instructions: Before each optimization run: check F1 (flow consistency), F4 (bounds), F10 (layout diversity). After a failure occurs: find the matching failure ID, add a new entry to its occurrences array with the full context (run_name, date, flows, error_message, what_was_done, resolved). If you encounter a failure pattern not covered here, create a new entry with the next available ID (F12, F13, ...) following the same schema.
